<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Implementing movie similarity**

I'm adding `getSimilarMovies` function to fetch similar movies from TMDB API. I'm handling edge cases for similar movies API, checking media type to avoid TV items in movie similarity requests. 

**Handling edge cases**

I'm implementing strict PSD 'last add wins' logic for movie similarity, using the last movie item's ID as the source. I'm using the last element in persisted order for movie similarity as per PSD, documenting that this may differ from TV items. I'm implementing `mostRecentMovieForSimilar` to use the last movie item as the anchor for similar rows, rather than potentially incorrect TV data. I'm adjusting the anchor item selection to use the last item in the array regardless of type, then conditionally fetch similar movies only when the last item is a movie. 

**Adjusting anchor item logic**

I'm using the last item in the array as the anchor item regardless of type, but will only fetch similar movies when the last item is actually a movie. I'm ensuring the API call for similar movies only happens when the anchor item is actually a movie, preventing incorrect requests for TV entries. I'm clarifying the filter interface to expose `filteredItems` for display while keeping `items` for internal use. 

I'm clarifying the hook to return filtered items, filter state, and similar movies data. I'm aligning the similar movies hook to return UseQueryResult<TmdbPagedMoviesResponse> for consistency with other list hooks. 

I'm restructuring the similar movies hook to return a nested object with `similar: { data, loading, error, refetch }` for consistent API design. I'm implementing filter functionality with 'all', 'movie', and 'tv' options, mapping 'tv' to 'Series' as per PSD. </details></think>

## Summary

### Files touched
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/hooks/useWatchlist.ts` | **New** — Zustand slice + filter state, derived `filteredItems`, similar fetch + `UseQueryResult`-shaped `similar`, `hydrated` gate for anchor. |
| `src/hooks/index.ts` | Export `useWatchlist`, `UseWatchlistResult`, `WatchlistMediaFilter`. |
| `src/api/movies.ts` | **`getSimilarMovies`** → `GET /movie/{id}/similar` via `client` only. |
| `src/api/types.ts` | **`WatchlistMediaFilter`**, **`UseWatchlistResult`**. |
| `src/utils/watchlistFilters.ts` | **New** — `filterWatchlistItems`, `getWatchlistSimilarMovieAnchorId` (pure, testable). |
| `__tests__/watchlistFilters.test.ts` | **New** — filter + anchor behaviour. |

### `useWatchlist()` return shape (`UseWatchlistResult`)

- **`hydrated`** — from `watchlistStore` (similar anchor is only computed after `hydrated` is true).
- **`count`**, **`items`** — full store list and count (unfiltered; order = persist / append order).
- **`filter`** — `'all' | 'movie' | 'tv'` (maps to UI **All / Movies / Series**).
- **`setFilter`** — updates filter only (no network).
- **`filteredItems`** — client-side slice of **`items`** by **`filter`**.
- **`similar`** — `UseQueryResult<TmdbPagedMoviesResponse>`: **`data`**, **`loading`**, **`error`**, **`refetch`** for **`GET /movie/{id}/similar`** only; idle (`data`/`error` cleared, `loading` false) when there is no anchor id.

### How “most recent” is defined

- **Recency** = **last entry in `items`**, i.e. the item most recently appended by **`addItem`** (store uses `[...state.items, item]`).
- **Similar call** uses that tail entry’s **`id` only if `mediaType === 'movie'`**, so the movie similar route is never called with a TV id. If the tail item is **`tv`**, the anchor is **`null`** and the similar query stays idle until the tail is a movie (e.g. after another add/remove).

`axios` is only used inside `src/api/client.ts` (unchanged); the hook goes through **`getSimilarMovies`** in **`movies.ts`**.

Tests: **`npm test`** and **`npm run lint`** both pass.

---

_**User**_

Attach: @docs/PSD-Watchlist.md @src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx @src/hooks/useWatchlist.ts @src/navigation/types.ts @src/utils/image.ts @rules/theming.mdc @rules/components.mdc @rules/navigation.mdc @rules/movielist-project.mdc

Out of scope: W3 tab badge, W4 empty state, W5 “Because you saved” row, W6 optimistic remove + filter-empty polish.

Deliverable: files changed; how filter + grid bind to the hook; navigate call for Details.

## Deliverable

### Files changed
| File | Purpose |
|------|--------|
| `src/navigation/types.ts` | `WatchlistStackParamList` now includes **`Detail: { movieId: number }`**. |
| `src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx` | Watchlist stack registers **`Detail`** (same `DetailScreen` as Search); **`WatchlistMain`** uses **`headerShown: false`** for the in-screen PSD header. |
| `src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx` | Route typing includes **`WatchlistStackParamList['Detail']`**. |
| `src/components/watchlist/WatchlistGridCard.tsx` | **New** — 2:3 poster (`contentCard` + `radiusCardOuter` / inner placeholder), rating badge (components.mdc pattern), **×** remove (wired to parent **no-op** until W6), **Details** button. |
| `src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx` | PSD §3 layout: collection label + **My Watchlist**, search/person **placeholders**, **All / Movies / Series** chips, **`FlatList`** `numColumns={2}` grid. |

### How filter + grid bind to `useWatchlist`
- The screen calls **`useWatchlist()`** once at the top (always, before any return).
- **`filter`** / **`setFilter`** drive the chips: each chip calls **`setFilter(chip.key)`** with **`WatchlistMediaFilter`** (`'all' | 'movie' | 'tv'` ↔ UI **All / Movies / Series**). Active chip uses **`colors.secondary_container`**; idle uses **`surface_container_highest`**.
- **`filteredItems`** is the **`FlatList`** `data` prop (client-side list only; no TMDB on the screen).
- Until **`hydrated`** is true, the screen shows a minimal **Loading…** state (no header/chips) so counts and filters are not shown on stale persist state.

### Details navigation
- For **`mediaType === 'movie'`**, **Details** calls:

```ts
navigation.navigate('Detail', { movieId: item.id });
```

on the **Watchlist** stack, so back returns to **`WatchlistMain`**.
- For **`tv`**, **Details** is **disabled** (`detailsEnabled={false}`): opening **`useMovieDetail`** with a TV id would be wrong until a TV detail flow exists.

### Remove control
- **×** calls a stable **`handleRemovePlaceholder`** no-op (commented for **W6**).

`npx tsc --noEmit`, **`npm run lint`**, and **`npm test`** all pass.

---

_**User**_

## Session — 2026-04-14 (W3)

Goal: PSD-Watchlist **§7 W3** + §6 — Watchlist **tab numeric badge** per @docs/PSD-Watchlist.md: visible when **`count > 0`** and store is **`hydrated`**; badge styling uses theme **`primary_container`** (no raw hex); count updates when the watchlist changes from **any** tab/screen.

Attach: @docs/PSD-Watchlist.md @docs/PSD-Architecture.md @src/navigation/ @src/store/watchlistStore.ts @src/theme/colors.ts @rules/navigation.mdc @rules/store.mdc @rules/movielist-project.mdc

Out of scope: W4 empty state, W5 similar row, W6 optimistic remove / filter-empty / Profile.

Deliverable: files changed; `tabBarBadge` vs custom icon approach; how `hydrated` hides the badge until safe.

---

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

## W3 — Watchlist tab badge

### Files changed
- **`src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx`** — `MainTabNavigator` subscribes to **`useWatchlistStore`** (`count`, **`hydrated`** via **`useShallow`**), sets **`tabBarBadge`** / **`tabBarBadgeStyle`** on the **Watchlist** `Tab.Screen`, and adds a **`StyleSheet`** for badge styling using **`colors.primary_container`** and **`colors.on_primary_container`** (no extra hex).

### `tabBarBadge` vs custom icon
- **Chosen: `tabBarBadge` + `tabBarBadgeStyle`** — matches PSD §6 (“`tabBarBadge` or custom icon”), keeps the existing **`tabBarIcon`** bookmark, and avoids duplicating layout/positioning for a pill on top of the icon.
- **Custom icon** would mean rendering the count inside a composite `tabBarIcon` component; that’s more code and easier to drift from platform tab metrics. **`tabBarBadge`** is the right default here.

### How **`hydrated`** hides the badge until it’s safe
- **`watchlistBadge`** is only set when **`hydrated && count > 0`**; otherwise it is **`undefined`**, so React Navigation **does not show** a badge.
- While persist is still rehydrating, **`hydrated`** is **`false`** in **`watchlistStore`**, so we never show a transient **`count`** (e.g. **0** before stored items load), which aligns with **`store.mdc`** / PSD §6.

### Reactivity from any tab
- **`useWatchlistStore`** is global; any screen that **`addItem`** / **`removeItem`** updates **`count`**, **`MainTabNavigator`** re-renders, and **`tabBarBadge`** updates without extra wiring.

**Display rule:** badge text is **`String(count)`**, or **`'99+'`** when **`count > 99`**.

`npx tsc --noEmit`, **`npm run lint`**, and **`npm test`** all succeed.

---

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

## W4 — Empty Watchlist

### Files changed
| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `src/api/types.ts` | Extended **`UseWatchlistResult`** with **`popularRecommendations: UseQueryResult<TmdbPagedMoviesResponse>`**. |
| `src/hooks/useWatchlist.ts` | When **`hydrated && count === 0`**, loads **`getTrendingMoviesWeek({ page: 1 })`** (from **`movies.ts`** only); clears that query when the watchlist is non-empty; exposes **`refetch`** via **`popularRecommendations`**. |
| `src/components/watchlist/WatchlistScreenHeader.tsx` | **New** — shared header (collection label, **My Watchlist**, optional **`countLine`**, actions, optional filter chips). |
| `src/components/watchlist/WatchlistEmptyState.tsx` | **New** — bookmark circle (**`secondary_container`**), headline + body, **Browse Trending Now** CTA, **POPULAR RECOMMENDATIONS** + skeleton / data / error + **Try again**. |
| `src/components/watchlist/WatchlistPopularSkeleton.tsx` | **New** — horizontal shimmer cards (theme **`ShimmerBox`** + **`homeRowCardWidth`** / **`contentCard.aspectRatio`**). |
| `src/components/watchlist/WatchlistBrowseTrendingCta.tsx` | **New** — **`primaryGradient`** fill via **`react-native-svg`** (no new deps). |
| `src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx` | Branches on **`hydrated`** / **`count`**; wires empty UI + tab switch + trending cards → **`Detail`**. |

### Empty vs populated
1. **`!hydrated`** — unchanged minimal **Loading…** (no TMDB-driven empty UI).
2. **`hydrated && count === 0`** — **`ScrollView`** with **`WatchlistScreenHeader`** (**`countLine="0 titles"`**, **`showChips={false}`**) and **`WatchlistEmptyState`** (bookmark, copy, CTA, recommendations block driven by **`popularRecommendations`**).
3. **`hydrated && count > 0`** — existing **`FlatList`** grid; header uses **`WatchlistScreenHeader`** with **`showChips`** (no count line).

The screen still **only** uses **`useWatchlist()`** for remote data (trending lives on the hook); **no `axios`** in the screen.

### Tab switch for **Browse Trending Now**
```ts
navigation.navigate('Home', { screen: 'HomeMain' });
```
Typed **`CompositeScreenProps`** tab + stack navigation: switches to the **Home** tab and targets **`HomeMain`** on the Home stack.

### Recommendations UX
- **Loading, no data** → **`WatchlistPopularSkeleton`**.
- **Error** → message + **Try again** → **`popularRecommendations.refetch`**.
- **Success** → horizontal **`ContentCard`** row from **`results`**; tap → **`navigation.navigate('Detail', { movieId })`** on the **Watchlist** stack.

`npx tsc --noEmit`, **`npm run lint`**, and **`npm test`** all pass.

---

_**User**_

Goal: PSD-Watchlist **§7 W5** + §3 — **“Because you saved [Title]”** row per @docs/PSD-Watchlist.md: only when watchlist has **≥ 1** item; title uses **most recently saved** item; horizontal **similar** cards from data **`useWatchlist`** already loads; **See All** in `primary_container`; **hide entire section** if similar is empty; navigate **See All** in a minimal way (reuse existing See All stack screen if present).

Attach: @docs/PSD-Watchlist.md @src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx @src/hooks/useWatchlist.ts @src/navigation/types.ts @rules/theming.mdc @rules/navigation.mdc @rules/movielist-project.mdc

Out of scope: W6 optimistic remove / filter-empty / Profile polish; changing how similar is fetched (hook already owns it unless broken).

Deliverable: files changed; visibility condition; See All navigation target + params.

## W5 — “Because you saved” row

### Files changed
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/navigation/types.ts` | **`SeeAllMode`** includes **`'similar'`**; **`SeeAll`** params add optional **`similarSourceMovieId`** (used when `mode === 'similar'`). |
| `src/hooks/useSeeAll.ts` | **`UseSeeAllInput.similarSourceMovieId`**; **`fetchSeeAllPage`** calls **`getSimilarMovies`** for **`similar`** (paginated like other modes). |
| `src/screens/SeeAllScreen.tsx` | Passes **`similarSourceMovieId`** into **`useSeeAll`**. |
| `src/components/watchlist/WatchlistBecauseYouSavedSection.tsx` | **New** — headline **`Because you saved {savedTitle}`**, **See All** (`**primary_container**` label), horizontal **`ContentCard`**s. |
| `src/screens/WatchlistScreen.tsx` | **`items`** + **`similar`** from **`useWatchlist`**; visibility + **`WatchlistBecauseYouSavedSection`** in **`FlatList`** header; **See All** → Home **`SeeAll`**. |

### Visibility condition
The block renders only when **all** of the following hold:

1. **`count >= 1`** (watchlist not empty).  
2. **`!similar.loading`** (initial similar request finished).  
3. **`similar.error == null`**.  
4. **`(similar.data?.results ?? []).length > 0`** (TMDB returned at least one similar movie — **hide the whole section if similar is empty**).  
5. **`getWatchlistSimilarMovieAnchorId(items) != null`** (there is a **movie** tail anchor for the similar call; aligns with existing hook behaviour when the last save is TV).

Copy uses the **most recently appended** watchlist item: **`items[items.length - 1]`** (trimmed **`title`**, fallback **`'this title'`**), per store order / PSD “most recently saved”.

### See All — navigation target and params
Cross-tab nested navigation to the existing Home stack **See All** screen:

```ts
navigation.navigate('Home', {
  screen: 'SeeAll',
  params: {
    title: `Because you saved ${savedTitle}`,
    mode: 'similar',
    similarSourceMovieId: similarAnchorMovieId,
  },
});
```

- **`similarAnchorMovieId`** = **`getWatchlistSimilarMovieAnchorId(items)`** (same anchor as **`useWatchlist`**’s similar fetch).  
- **`useSeeAll`** loads **`GET /movie/{similarSourceMovieId}/similar`** with pagination.

`npx tsc --noEmit`, **`npm run lint`**, and **`npm test`** all pass.
